content.question: If parents and families are given the choice to move students to alternative selective mechanisms (private schools, church-converted tracks, inter-municipal transfers), what fraction of the sorting behavior currently attributed to 6/8-year gimnáziums will be displaced rather than eliminated by structural removal of the tracks?
content.reduction_path: Comparative policy analysis of education systems that have phased out early tracking (e.g., German länder, Nordic countries) to measure displacement of selective demand; modeling or pilot study of Hungarian parent choice behavior under hypothetical removal scenarios; administrative data on private-school and inter-municipal transfer trends following any future track-system changes.
